name: Uqba ibn al-Salt
title: Loyal Juhani
shortDescription: A brave man from the Juhaynah tribe who joined the Imam at the station of Juhaynah and remained loyal until the last moment in Karbala.
fullStory:
Uqba ibn al-Salt al-Juhani was a brave and pious man from the Juhaynah tribe. When Imam Hussain (AS) departed from Medina to Mecca and then towards Kufa, he passed through a station called 'Juhaynah' (the residence of this tribe) along the way.

Uqba, along with several others from his tribe, including Majma' ibn Ziyad al-Juhani, upon seeing Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan and recognizing his righteousness, decided to join this luminous caravan.

Many of those who had joined the Imam along the way left the caravan upon hearing the news of Muslim ibn Aqeel's martyrdom and losing hope of apparent victory. But Uqba ibn al-Salt was among those who remained by the Imam with firm faith.

On the day of Ashura, with the start of Umar Sa'd's army's all-out attack in the morning (the first attack), Uqba drew his sword shoulder to shoulder with the other Ansar of Hussain and, after a valiant defense of Wilayah, was martyred.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى عُقْبَةَ بْنِ الصَّلْتِ الجُهَنِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Uqba ibn al-Salt al-Juhani.
